title: dir formats support for `podman image scp`
body:
### Feature request description

Support directory formats (**oci-dir**, **docker-dir**) in `podman image scp`, so digest references can be preserved when both hosts run matching Podman versions.

**podman save** supports four formats: **oci-archive**, **oci-dir**, **docker-archive**, **docker-dir**. Today `podman image scp` only supports the archive formats (oci-archive, docker-archive): save to a single file -> transfer -> load on remote. That flow does not preserve digests. Adding dir formats would allow a more accurate transfer (tar dir -> transfer tarball -> untar on remote -> load from dir).


### Suggest potential solution

- Add **oci-dir** and **docker-dir** as allowed values for `podman image scp --format`.
- On the source: save image to a temp dir (e.g. `podman save --format oci-dir`), tar the directory, transfer the tarball over SSH.
- On the remote: untar the tarball, then load from the unpacked directory. This change incorporates teaching **podman --remote image load** to support dir formats (the remote client today may not support loading from oci-dir/docker-dir).
- Document that dir formats are for environments with matching/compatible Podman versions (dir layout stability across versions may be a concern).

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Using a registry (push/pull) is already preferred for normal use; this is for SCP-only flows where digest preservation is needed.


### Additional context

- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28234

comments.body: Is this something you're working on @Honny1?
Not at the moment. Feel free to take it!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
